content:
@Miguel Ojeda I sent phase 2 as two different series; lmk if these look reasonable:

  • https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250709-core-cstr-fanout-1-v1-0-64308e7203fc@gmail.com/
  • https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250709-core-cstr-fanout-1-v1-0-fd793b3e58a2@gmail.com/

apologies for similar message IDs, poor use of b4 on my part.

cc @Alice Ryhl
I've now sent v14 of the CStr migration series as step 3/5 in the migration. See https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250710-cstr-core-v14-0-ca7e0ca82c82@gmail.com/. cc @Benno Lossin
I've now sent series 4/5 as well: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250710-core-cstr-cstrings-v1-0-027420ea799e@gmail.com/. 17 tiny patches.
Attempted to send series 4/5 and hit gmail email limits. Can't email anymore today. Will try again tomorrow.
I can recommend Proton!
Or a kernel account...
Yeah I have one now, but I didn't want to switch mid-stream.
@Miguel Ojeda do you still think these patches can go through the rust tree? I've held off on rebasing and resending because there hasn't been any indication that they'll be taken.

Yeah, we should still pick at least one step, right? If you can rebase on what we have at the moment on rust-next, that would be great. I can pick them through this week (I will be busy with Kangrejos).
Thanks a lot!
Yep one step would be great. I'm traveling until 24 September without the relevant computer but I'll do this as soon as I can. Thanks Miguel!
I think 24th september is too late for this cycle?
I wouldn't say no to some help. I won't be able to do it before then I'm afraid

Your last step 2a is v3, which is on top of v6.17-rc1, so what I thought back then was that there shouldn't be many changes anyway. Since you mentioned rebasing, I always appreciate a trivial series to apply, especially if you noticed big changes, but it may be fine as it is. I can take a look.

i.e. this one: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250813-core-cstr-fanout-1-v3-0-a15eca059c51@gmail.com/

By the way, there was a comment from Benno.
And 2b is https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250813-core-cstr-fanout-1-v3-0-545c14bc44ff@gmail.com/, similar story.
I just took a quick look, and they indeed rebase cleanly.
We may have conflicts with next, of course, but that is to be expected.
@Tamir Duberstein Or did I miss something and you wanted to rebase/resubmit for some reason?
I don't think so!
Great, thanks!
Then let me quickly test 2a and 2b.
If they are clean then fine by me. I thought I had replied to Benno's question, I can do that when I get back to my computer in a bit - but the answer is indeed that we can't use write!
Ah I replied but I was still being throttled by Gmail, lol.
Oh :/ Perhaps consider kernel.org, at least for series submission, so that those don't count.
Yeah I'll start using it. Did they test clean?
Thanks! Do you think taking 3 this cycle would make sense? https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250813-cstr-core-v15-0-c732d9223f4e@gmail.com/
Rebased 3 and 4 and sent both. Would be amazing if either or both could be taken this cycle.
@Miguel Ojeda series 3 v17: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20251015-cstr-core-v17-0-dc5e7aec870d@gmail.com/. Sadly it went from 3 patches to 11.
Thanks Tamir, no worries, it looks fine, we will make it work.
@Andreas Hindborg your tags would probably be helpful on https://lore.kernel.org/all/20251015-cstr-core-v17-5-dc5e7aec870d@gmail.com/
I'll take a look in a few hours. I thought I already gave my ok for that one. Is it new, or did you drop my tags?
Thanks for the ping :)

Andreas Hindborg said:

I'll take a look in a few hours. I thought I already gave my ok for that one. Is it new, or did you drop my tags?

It's new, I think your tags were on a prior series. Thanks :folded_hands:

Tamir Duberstein said:

Andreas Hindborg said:

I'll take a look in a few hours. I thought I already gave my ok for that one. Is it new, or did you drop my tags?

It's new, I think your tags were on a prior series. Thanks :folded_hands:

I was missing drivers/block/rnull/* in my lei query :man_facepalming:
do you not have bs:"Andreas Hindborg"?

Benno Lossin said:

do you not have bs:"Andreas Hindborg"?

I do not. What is the bs field?
body + subject
ah but I guess it doesn't trigger in this case

Benno Lossin said:

ah but I guess it doesn't trigger in this case

Good point having it anyway though.
@Alice Ryhl thanks for the extra patches! I just tried sending v18 but hit gmail throttle again. Sigh. I'll have to prune the CC list. Will try again Monday.
I can send them for you if you want
Probably doesn't make it easier since it has to be a single series, but if you think it would help: https://github.com/tamird/linux/commits/b4/cstr-core/
Is that link up to date?
I just pushed, sorry, it took a while. They're there now
I'll have to add a Signed-off-by if I send them, but I can. Totally up to you, though.
If you're comfortable with it, that would be a-ok by me
I see you already sent it partially
Right yeah that's how I hit the limits =/
ok done, I finished the one you sent
Thanks a lot!
You're welcome, and thanks for doig the migration :)

Let's fix the root issue, please... We cannot be sending patches for everyone that hits limits or other issues in the infrastructure.

Did you try in the end kernel.org instead?
I haven't yet, because I started this series before i had the @kernel.org address. Next time I'll bite the bullet, rewrite the commits, and send from @kernel.org.
you don't need to rewrite the SoB's in order to send them from a different mail

Indeed, there is no need to rewrite (but that should not be too bad if you wanted to do it for some reason).

Can you please try to resend with the kernel.org one? Thanks!
Would you mind walking me through it? Do I need to add From? Is there a way to do this correctly with b4?
It should be done automatically for you -- please feel free to do a test run in private to e.g. yourself or me etc.
At least git-send-email adds the From line automatically
i.e. you should see that the email sent does have the From: line when it doesn't match the commit author
Yeah no extra steps are needed to send commits by others
ok will do it shortly
i'm not sure b4 will correctly flag it as a resend though
You set it up like normal. When you send the email, b4 or git notices the commit author isn't the same, and includes the necessary things.
Yeah it's not going to say "resend" but I can send it. Is that OK?
you can add the --resend option?
it refuses to resend v18 because it was never tagged. it will resend v17.
or does that one not work?

tamird@Mac linux % b4 send --resend v18
CRITICAL: Failed to convert tag to patches: No such tag: sent/cstr-core-v18
tamird@Mac linux % b4 send --resend
Converted the tag to 12 messages
---
...
---
  [PATCH RESEND v17 00/11] rust: replace kernel::str::CStr w/ core::ffi::CStr
  [PATCH RESEND v17 01/11] samples: rust: platform: remove trailing commas
  [PATCH RESEND v17 02/11] rust_binder: remove trailing comma
  [PATCH RESEND v17 03/11] rust_binder: use `kernel::fmt`
  [PATCH RESEND v17 04/11] rust_binder: use `core::ffi::CStr` method names
  [PATCH RESEND v17 05/11] rnull: use `kernel::fmt`
  [PATCH RESEND v17 06/11] rust: alloc: use `kernel::fmt`
  [PATCH RESEND v17 07/11] rust: debugfs: use `kernel::fmt`
  [PATCH RESEND v17 08/11] rust: pci: use `kernel::fmt`
  [PATCH RESEND v17 09/11] rust: remove spurious `use core::fmt::Debug`
  [PATCH RESEND v17 10/11] rust: support formatting of foreign types
  [PATCH RESEND v17 11/11] rust: replace `CStr` with `core::ffi::CStr`
---
Ready to:
  - send the above messages to actual listed recipients
  - with envelope-from: Tamir Duberstein <tamird@kernel.org>
  - via SMTP server mail.kernel.org

Press Enter to proceed or Ctrl-C to abort^C
Try adding RESEND using --set-prefixes so that it thinks it's sending v18 normally
apparnetly there's --manual-reroll
Sent!
I assume that was the test run?
oh, damn it. It was the pruned CC list because I was preparing for another gmail send. Here I go again...
Still didn't work properly. b4 send -o tmp produced a very long list of recipients, but the real send did not
weird
the resend is not respecting the recipients in the cover letter at all it seems?
can you push it to the branch again?
oh, no, the problem is the manual reroll doesn't pull the recipient list
i think i can overwrite the tag, sec
ok, here i go

OK, should be correct now. Yeah, b4 prep --manual-reroll + b4 send --resend does not work as you'd expect.

https://github.com/mricon/b4/issues/69

Hi folks, the next step in this migration is

there is no flag day in this one, and the patches could be taken through any tree. do folks have suggests on how best to send these? obviously it's easiest for me to send a single series while it's easier for maintainers if i send one per tree -- sadly i don't know of any tooling that makes this easier

Yeah, split would be easier for everyone to pick it at their own pace...

git-send-email and allows passing a range of commits which you can combine with things like --to-cmd to run a script to get the To/Cc's plus --no-cover-letter --no-thread --no-numbered or similar. Also git-format-patch supports similar options.

Worst case, you could do something like git rebase --exec calling git-format-patch / scripts/get_maintainer.pl / git-send-email.

Another option is to send it as one series and I could pick those that maintainers Ack, and then hopefully it gets reduced to just a few that we can send independently later.
(And thanks for keeping this up!)

The other complication is that I probably want to base my series on those maintainers' next branches, which means I need something like my patch -> find tree -> find branch -> rebase [-> rewrite] -> send

I guess they may ask for a rebase in that case, i.e. if there are non-trivial conflicts (you need to wait until -rc1, right? So hopefully not many conflicts)

You could also do a mix, i.e. send what you have on top of rust-next now, we try to apply a few that people may reply to with an Ack in the next week, and after -rc1 you do the rest independently since now they can apply them.
hmm do I need to wait until rc1? i think i can send these as soon as stable is out, no?
oh, yeah, i need to wait until rc1

Miguel Ojeda said:

You could also do a mix, i.e. send what you have on top of rust-next now, we try to apply a few that people may reply to with an Ack in the next week, and after -rc1 you do the rest independently since now they can apply them.

sounds like a good idea
i'll send the ones that go to the rust tree now
turns out these patches already have tags
hey @Miguel Ojeda, does https://lore.kernel.org/all/20251117-core-cstr-cstrings-v4-1-924886ad9f75@gmail.com/ look worth taking, to you?
Yes, definitely, thanks for sending the updated one. I will put that one together with a couple from Benno today.
